title: atractylis japonica
intro: Atractylis japonica, also known as Japanese thistle or Hare's ear, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve pain. The plant's roots and leaves are rich in sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, offering potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving immune function. Atractylis japonica can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, which are often used to treat digestive issues, fever, and rheumatism.
